title: Tetracyclines
body_html:
Key Points
  • Tetracyclines are used in the management of a broad range of conditions - Chlamydia, Lyme disease, Brucella, Q-fever, Acne, Respiratory infections and more.
  • They can cause teeth staining and dental hypoplasia in developing children, therefore should be avoided in children under 12 years and breast feeding mothers.
  • Minocycline has the broadest spectrum of activity.
3
4059
Pharmacology
Antimicrobials
Key Points
  • Tetracyclines are used in the management of a broad range of conditions - Chlamydia, Lyme disease, Brucella, Q-fever, Acne, Respiratory infections and more.
  • They can cause teeth staining and dental hypoplasia in developing children, therefore should be avoided in children under 12 years and breast feeding mothers.
  • Minocycline has the broadest spectrum of activity.




Additional Content

Tetracyclines are a class of antibiotics which are commonly used in clinical practice.

Examples
  • doxycycline
  • tetracycline

Mechanism of action
  • protein synthesis inhibitors
  • binds to 30S subunit blocking binding of aminoacyl-tRNA

Mechanism of resistance
  • increased efflux of the bacteria by plasmid-encoded transport pumps, ribosomal protection

Indications
  • acne vulgaris
  • Lyme disease
  • Chlamydia
-Mycoplasma pneumoniae

Adverse effects
  • discolouration of teeth
  • photosensitivity
